story: Tonight I sit across from him, too late to turn back. Earlier, instead of going to my shift, I drove to the storage unit and took out what I had hidden there: a small vial meant to vanish in whiskey. He comes in, loosens his tie, and, as always, “Pour me a glass of poison!” I bring the whiskey. He asks why I skipped my shift—Elizabeth has told him. I lie about a migraine and a prescription. The house is spotless; my face calm.
implausible_knowledge: Earlier, instead of going to my shift, I drove to the storage unit and took out what I had hidden there: a small vial meant to vanish in whiskey
question: What will he most plausibly do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Push the glass away and refuse to drink.
answer_without_knowledge: Lift the tumbler and drink the whiskey.
